subsystem: ImageMagick MPC (Magick Persistent Cache) coder — WriteMPCImage(), the encoder that serializes an in-memory Image structure to the .mpc/.cache persistent-cache format. It writes a textual meta-information header (id, signature, class, colors, geometry, resolution, page, profiles, properties, montage directory) followed by a raw colormap blob for PseudoClass images, then persists the pixel cache to a sibling .cache file.
provenance: Identified by the ImageMagick public API idioms and type names: MagickBooleanType/MagickCoreSignature, ImageInfo/Image/ExceptionInfo, MagickPathExtent-sized buffers, OpenBlob/WriteBlob/WriteBlobString/CloseBlob, FormatLocaleString, AcquireQuantumMemory/RelinquishMagickMemory, PersistPixelCache, ThrowWriterException, and the literal marker 'id=MagickCache' written at line 55. The banner names the file corpus/eval/eval_0071_cwe772.c and the header comment 'sample: primevul_c_train_2432_fixed' marks this as the post-fix revision; the function is verbatim ImageMagick coders/mpc.c:WriteMPCImage.
trust_boundary: This is an ENCODER, not a parser, so the bytes it emits are not themselves attacker input. However every field it reads comes from the in-memory Image object, which is routinely populated by DECODING an untrusted input image earlier in the pipeline. Thus image->colors, image->depth, image->colormap[], image->profiles, image->properties, image->montage, image->directory, and the geometry/resolution/page scalars are all attacker-influenceable. The hostile surface here is therefore (1) resource lifetime on the allocations this function makes while processing those fields (the open blob and the colormap allocation), and (2) dereferences/index bounds driven by attacker-controlled counts (image->colors vs image->colormap length, and profile lookups). The fixed-size stack buffers are written only through bounded FormatLocaleString/CopyMagickString calls and are not a live overflow surface.

what_the_code_does.explanation: The function signature takes the write settings (image_info), the image list head to encode (image), and an exception sink. It returns MagickBooleanType (MagickTrue/MagickFalse). Two fixed-size stack char arrays of MagickPathExtent bytes are declared: 'buffer' is a scratch line-formatting buffer reused for every meta-information line, and 'cache_filename' holds the derived name of the sibling .cache pixel-cache file. 'property'/'value' are borrowed (non-owning) pointers into the image's property table. 'status' carries success/failure across blob operations. 'offset'/'scene' track the persistent-cache write offset and the frame index. 'i' is the signed loop counter used for colormap and value-escaping loops. 'depth' holds the per-frame quantum depth and 'imageListLength' the number of frames for progress reporting.

Entry contract checks: the three assert()s confirm the pointers are non-NULL and carry the correct MagickCoreSignature (a self-integrity marker) — these guard against internal misuse, not untrusted data. If debugging is on, the filename is logged with a proper '%s' format (no format-string hazard). OpenBlob opens the destination file/stream in binary write mode; this is the first OWNED resource acquired. On failure the function returns immediately at line 40 — safe, because nothing was successfully opened yet. From this point until CloseBlob at line 426, an open blob is a live resource that every early exit must release.

The output filename is copied into cache_filename with an explicit bound of MagickPathExtent-6, deliberately reserving 6 bytes so AppendImageFormat can append the '.cache' extension without overflowing the MagickPathExtent-sized array. CopyMagickString is ImageMagick's length-bounded strlcpy analogue, so this copy truncates rather than overflows. AppendImageFormat replaces/adds the extension to form the pixel-cache sibling filename. scene/offset are initialized to 0 and imageListLength is fetched for the progress callback denominator.

The do/while loop iterates over the frames of the image list (its termination at line 425 depends on image_info->adjoin). For each frame it computes the quantum depth (8/16/32/64). It then demotes a palette image to DirectClass when the declared colormap size exceeds the representable quantum range — a consistency correction so an over-large colors count does not later drive a bogus colormap. Note 'depth' computed here is the value later switched on when serializing the colormap.

The header block begins with the fixed magic line 'id=MagickCache'. Each subsequent line is composed into 'buffer' via FormatLocaleString, which is a size-bounded (snprintf-style) formatter capped at MagickPathExtent, then flushed with WriteBlobString. The class/colors/alpha-trait, channel counts, and columns/rows/depth are emitted. Enum values (storage_class, alpha_trait) are mapped through CommandOptionToMnemonic to human-readable tokens; numeric fields are printed as doubles with %.20g. Because every FormatLocaleString call passes MagickPathExtent as the size, over-long expansions truncate into 'buffer' rather than overflow it.

A long series of optional meta-lines follows the same pattern: test whether a field is non-default (type, colorspace, intensity, endian, compression/quality, units, resolution, page geometry, tile-offset spanning lines 73-134), and if so format it into 'buffer' and write it. Every one of these uses the same bounded FormatLocaleString(buffer,MagickPathExtent,...) idiom, so none is a buffer-overflow surface. The page block chooses between a full page= form (line 116) and a shorter offset-only form (line 124) depending on which page fields are set. These lines are metadata reflection with no allocation or lifetime consequences.

Animation/sequence metadata. When the frame is part of a multi-image list, one combined line carrying iterations/delay/ticks-per-second (and scene when non-zero) is emitted. Otherwise (else branch, lines 150-176) each of scene, iterations, delay, ticks-per-second is written individually only when non-default. All formatting is again bounded to MagickPathExtent.

First profile pass: it enumerates every attached profile by NAME and writes a 'profile=<name>' line for each. Crucially this loop looks up 'profile=GetImageProfile(image,name)' and GUARDS it with 'if (profile != NULL)' at line 242 before doing anything with it. This defensive NULL check is the correct pattern and stands in contrast to the second profile pass at lines 303-325, which omits it. After the profiles, an optional 'montage=' line is written (lines 251-256).

Property serialization. For each property, the key is written as '<name>=' (the property key is bounded into 'buffer', truncating if enormous). The value is fetched and, if present, its length taken with strlen. The loop at 270-272 scans for the first whitespace character. If the value contains no whitespace and is non-empty, it is written raw; otherwise it is wrapped in braces '{...}' and any literal '}' inside is backslash-escaped so the value round-trips. All value bytes go to the dynamic blob via WriteBlob/WriteBlobByte using the exact strlen length, so there is no fixed-buffer overflow here; the escaping is a correctness/format concern, not a memory-safety one.

A form-feed/NUL separator marks the end of the text header. If a montage is present, the tile directory string is written followed by a NUL terminator. Then the SECOND profile pass writes the actual profile payloads: for each profile name it looks up the StringInfo, writes its length as an MSB long, and writes the raw datum. Note that unlike the first pass (line 242), this loop calls GetStringInfoLength(profile) at line 320 WITHOUT first checking 'profile != NULL' — the length accessor dereferences the StringInfo, so a NULL return from GetImageProfile would fault here.

For palette images the colormap is serialized. packet_size = 3*depth/8 gives the byte-width of one RGB triple at the current depth (12 bytes at depth 32, 6 at 16, 3 at 8). AcquireQuantumMemory(image->colors, packet_size) allocates image->colors entries of packet_size bytes each; AcquireQuantumMemory performs its own multiplication-overflow check, so the size math is not a heap-overflow primitive. This 'colormap' heap buffer is the SECOND owned resource. If allocation fails the function returns MagickFalse at line 342. The write pointer 'q' is set to the start of the buffer and the loop walks i over image->colors.

Per colormap entry a switch on 'depth' selects the packing width. The 32/16/8 cases pack red/green/blue via PopLongPixel/PopShortPixel/PopCharPixel, advancing 'q' by exactly packet_size bytes per iteration, so total bytes written equal packet_size*image->colors — matching the allocation exactly with no overrun. The 'default:' case (unsupported depth) is the resource-cleanup path this fixed revision cares about: it FREES the colormap with RelinquishMagickMemory BEFORE calling ThrowWriterException, which is the correction versus the pre-fix code that leaked the buffer. After the loop, WriteBlob(image,packet_size*image->colors,colormap) flushes the buffer (line 404) and RelinquishMagickMemory frees it on the normal path (line 405).

After the meta-header and colormap, the pixel data is persisted to cache_filename via PersistPixelCache. On failure ThrowWriterException is invoked (which closes the blob and returns). If there is no next frame the loop breaks; otherwise it advances to the next image and optionally calls the progress monitor, which may cancel the write (break). The loop repeats while adjoin is set (multi-frame). On normal exit CloseBlob releases the blob and the final status is returned. CloseBlob at line 426 is the single normal release site for the blob resource acquired at line 38.

what_could_go_wrong.explanation: This is a resource-leak (CWE-772) escape hatch. The blob was opened at line 38 and is only released by CloseBlob (line 426) or by the ThrowWriterException macro (which closes it). This bare 'return(MagickFalse)' takes neither path: when AcquireQuantumMemory fails — reachable by an attacker-influenced image->colors that is large enough to exhaust the allocator on a PseudoClass frame — the function returns with the destination blob still OPEN. The attacker controls the trigger (a crafted palette image with a very large colors count converted/written repeatedly), and the impact is a leaked file/stream handle per failed write, i.e. handle/descriptor exhaustion and denial of service under repeated conversions. The invariant the implementation must uphold is: every exit after line 38 must release the blob; this path should be 'ThrowWriterException(ResourceLimitError,...)' (or an explicit CloseBlob before return) rather than a bare return. Note the sibling fix at line 353 correctly frees the colormap before throwing, so the colormap itself does not leak here (it is NULL); the leaked object is the blob.

NULL-pointer dereference (CWE-476). In this second profile pass the result of GetImageProfile is passed straight into GetStringInfoLength(profile) and GetStringInfoDatum(profile) without a NULL check, whereas the first pass at line 242 explicitly guards 'if (profile != (StringInfo *) NULL)'. GetStringInfoLength dereferences profile->length, so if GetImageProfile returns NULL for a name yielded by the iterator (e.g. a profile entry whose value was removed or is NULL, an attacker-influenceable profile table state), the write faults and crashes the process (DoS). The invariant is that the two profile passes must apply the SAME NULL discipline; the fix is to wrap the write in the same 'if (profile != NULL)' guard used at line 242. This is a latent crash whose reachability depends on the profile map ever holding a name with a NULL StringInfo.

Out-of-bounds read (CWE-125), latent. The loop indexes image->colormap[i] for i in [0, image->colors). The destination 'colormap' heap buffer is sized to image->colors entries, so the WRITE side is exactly bounded. The risk is on the READ side: image->colormap[i] assumes the source colormap array actually contains image->colors elements. If an earlier decoder set image->colors larger than the number of entries it actually allocated in image->colormap (a classic count/array mismatch from an untrusted image), this loop over-reads the colormap array and serializes out-of-bounds heap memory into the cache file (information disclosure). The invariant — image->colors <= number of allocated image->colormap entries — is owned by the decoder/SetImageColormap path, not by this function, so this is a contract to confirm upstream rather than a bug in the shown lines.

Signed-cast edge case (CWE-190/CWE-125, low). image->colors is size_t but the loop bound is '(ssize_t) image->colors'. If image->colors ever exceeds SSIZE_MAX the cast becomes negative and the loop body never executes, leaving 'colormap' fully UNINITIALIZED; yet line 404 then writes packet_size*image->colors bytes of that buffer to the blob, leaking uninitialized heap contents (information disclosure). This requires an implausibly large colors value (>2^63) that AcquireQuantumMemory would almost certainly reject first, so it is a theoretical hygiene note rather than a practical exploit, but the mixed signed/unsigned handling of image->colors is worth hardening.

Assertion-based validation elided under NDEBUG (CWE-617/CWE-20, low). These asserts are the only NULL/integrity checks on image_info, image, and exception. In release builds (NDEBUG) they compile to nothing, so if any caller ever passes a NULL/uninitialized handle these guards vanish and the subsequent unconditional dereferences crash. In practice these are internal invariants for a coder entrypoint and callers uphold them, so this is a defensive-coding note; it is not a primary attack surface for a writer.

summary: A faithful copy of ImageMagick's MPC encoder WriteMPCImage. The fixed-buffer formatting is uniformly bounded (FormatLocaleString/CopyMagickString), so it has no live stack-overflow surface, and the flagged CWE-772 in the colormap default case is correctly remediated (RelinquishMagickMemory before ThrowWriterException at line 353). Two genuine residual issues remain: the colormap-allocation-failure path at line 342 returns without releasing the open blob (a real CWE-772 handle leak), and the second profile loop dereferences GetImageProfile()'s result without the NULL check its sibling loop applies (CWE-476). Overall risk is low-to-medium and confined to DoS/leak, since this is an encoder rather than a parser.

reviewed_anchors.reason: Written only via size-bounded FormatLocaleString(...,MagickPathExtent,...) calls that truncate; no unbounded copy targets it.
Filled by CopyMagickString with an explicit MagickPathExtent-6 bound reserving room for the appended extension; bounded, truncating.
Length-bounded strlcpy analogue; the -6 reserve prevents the subsequent AppendImageFormat from overflowing.
Operates on the MagickPathExtent buffer whose copy was pre-bounded to leave extension room; standard bounded ImageMagick helper.
Blob is a live owned resource; the line-342 early return does not release it (CWE-772).
Only NULL/signature validation; compiled out under NDEBUG (CWE-617), low severity for a coder entrypoint.
Constant-width arithmetic on a small enumerated depth (8/16/32); no attacker-controlled overflow.
Owned heap allocation whose failure path (line 342) leaks the open blob; allocation itself is overflow-checked.
Returns without CloseBlob — resource leak of the open blob (CWE-772).
Read of image->colormap assumes it holds image->colors entries; count/array mismatch → OOB read (CWE-125), latent/upstream invariant.
This is the applied fix: colormap is freed before throwing, and ThrowWriterException also closes the blob; correctly handled.
Length equals the allocation size; the packing loop advances q by exactly packet_size per entry, so no over-read of the destination on supported depths.

reviewed_anchors.anchor: First profile loop with NULL guard (lines 239-249)
reviewed_anchors.reason: Correctly checks 'if (profile != NULL)' before use; safe reference model for the second loop.

reviewed_anchors.anchor: Second profile loop GetStringInfoLength(profile) without NULL check (lines 318-320)
reviewed_anchors.reason: Dereferences GetImageProfile result without the NULL guard its sibling uses → CWE-476 NULL dereference.
All bytes written to the dynamic blob using exact strlen length; format/correctness concern, not a fixed-buffer memory-safety issue.
Failure path uses ThrowWriterException which closes the blob and returns; resource-clean.
Standard multi-frame iteration; break paths reach CloseBlob; no lifetime defect in the loop control itself.

findings.title: Open blob leaked when colormap allocation fails
findings.bug_class: Missing release of resource (open blob) on error path
findings.invariant: Every function exit after OpenBlob at line 38 must release the blob via CloseBlob or a ThrowWriterException macro that closes it; no bare return may leave the destination blob open.
findings.failure_mode: On a PseudoClass frame, when AcquireQuantumMemory (line 339, driven by attacker-influenced image->colors) fails, control takes the bare 'return(MagickFalse)' which skips both CloseBlob (line 426) and the throw macros. The destination file/stream handle opened at line 38 leaks. Repeated conversions of such images exhaust file descriptors/handles, a denial-of-service. Should be ThrowWriterException(ResourceLimitError,...) or an explicit CloseBlob before return.
findings.what_to_confirm: Confirm OpenBlob acquired a handle by this point and that no outer caller closes the blob after a MagickFalse return; verify other ImageMagick coders use ThrowWriterException here rather than a bare return.

findings.title: NULL-pointer dereference in second profile loop (missing profile guard)
findings.invariant: Any StringInfo obtained from GetImageProfile must be NULL-checked before GetStringInfoLength/GetStringInfoDatum dereference it — the same discipline the first profile loop applies at line 242.
findings.failure_mode: If GetImageProfile returns NULL for a name yielded by the profile iterator (a profile entry with a NULL/absent StringInfo, an attacker-influenceable profile-table state carried over from decoding), GetStringInfoLength(profile) dereferences a NULL StringInfo and crashes the process (DoS). The asymmetry with the guarded first loop (line 242) is the tell.
findings.what_to_confirm: Confirm whether GetImageProfile can return NULL for a name returned by GetNextImageProfile in the same iteration; if so, wrap the writes in 'if (profile != (const StringInfo *) NULL)' to match line 242.

findings.title: Colormap read assumes image->colors matches allocated colormap length
findings.anchor: for (i=0; i < (ssize_t) image->colors; i++) reading image->colormap[i]
findings.where_it_lives: implementation (source image->colormap allocated in decoder / SetImageColormap path, file not provided)
findings.invariant: image->colors must never exceed the number of PixelInfo entries actually allocated in image->colormap.
findings.failure_mode: The loop serializes image->colormap[i].red/green/blue for i up to image->colors. The destination heap buffer is correctly sized, but if a decoder set image->colors larger than the real colormap array (a hostile palette image), this loop over-reads adjacent heap memory and writes it into the .mpc/.cache file — an information disclosure. This function does not establish that invariant itself; it must be enforced where the colormap is allocated.
findings.what_to_confirm: Verify every path that sets image->colors also (re)allocates image->colormap to at least that many entries; check SetImageColormap/AcquireImageColormap usage in the decoders that feed this writer.

findings.title: Entry validation relies on assert(), removed under NDEBUG
findings.bug_class: Validation compiled out (reachable assertion / missing input validation)
findings.invariant: The pointer/signature preconditions guarded by these asserts must actually hold, or be enforced with real runtime checks, before the unconditional dereferences that follow (e.g. image->debug at line 34).
findings.failure_mode: In release builds compiled with NDEBUG the asserts expand to nothing, so a caller passing a NULL or corrupt image/image_info/exception is dereferenced without a guard and crashes. For a coder entrypoint these are internal invariants normally upheld by callers, so impact is limited; noted for defensive-coding completeness.
findings.what_to_confirm: Confirm all invocation paths guarantee non-NULL, correctly-signed arguments, or replace the asserts with real return-on-error checks.

audit_checklist: Replace the bare 'return(MagickFalse)' at line 342 with a blob-closing exit (ThrowWriterException(ResourceLimitError,...) or explicit CloseBlob) so the OpenBlob handle from line 38 is never leaked.
Add the same 'if (profile != NULL)' guard used at line 242 to the second profile loop (lines 318-322) before GetStringInfoLength/GetStringInfoDatum to eliminate the NULL dereference.
Verify upstream that image->colors can never exceed the allocated length of image->colormap, since the loop at line 347 reads image->colormap[i] for the full colors count.

Confirm AcquireQuantumMemory(image->colors, packet_size) performs the multiplication-overflow check for all reachable depth/colors combinations and that the depth switch always resolves to 8/16/32 (default frees + throws).

Check the signed cast '(ssize_t) image->colors' cannot skip the colormap fill loop while line 404 still writes packet_size*image->colors bytes (uninitialized-memory disclosure on absurd colors values).

Ensure all other error exits (PersistPixelCache failure, progress-monitor cancel, unsupported depth) release both the colormap and the blob exactly once — no double-free of colormap given it is freed at lines 353 and 405.

Confirm none of the FormatLocaleString call sites can be coaxed into a format-string issue (all use fixed literals with MagickPathExtent bound) and that property/value bytes are correctly brace-escaped for round-trip.
